name: Cocoa Almond Cookies
recipeInstructions: In a large bowl, beat together butter and cream cheese until well blended. Add sugar; beat well. Add egg, vanilla, and almond extract; beat until blended. Stir together flour, cocoa, baking powder, and baking soda; gradually add to the butter mixture, beating until well combined. Divide dough into four portions. Shape each portion into a ball. Place one ball on wax paper; flatten into a round. Repeat with remaining balls. Wrap dough in plastic wrap; refrigerate about 6 hours or overnight. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Working with one portion at a time, place dough on a lightly floured surface. Roll each portion until it is 1/4 inch thick. Cut into 2-inch diameter rounds using a cookie cutter. Place cutouts onto an ungreased baking sheet. Bake for 6 to 8 minutes or until set. Remove from the baking sheet and let cool completely on a wire rack. Prepare the cocoa glaze by melting butter in a small saucepan over low heat. Add cocoa and water, stirring until thickened. Do not boil. Remove from heat; gradually stir powdered sugar, vanilla extract, and almond extract (if desired) until smooth. Use immediately to drizzle over the tops of the cookies. Garnish with chopped or sliced almonds. Makes about 4 dozen cookies.
